features.description: YAML 1.2 parser for .yoke.yml: AJV validation against yoke-config.schema.json with additionalProperties:false, version pin check (version:"1" required), relative-path resolution at load time (template paths, artifact schema paths, hook paths), ResolvedConfig typed output consumed by all downstream modules.
acceptance_criteria: Valid .yoke.yml with all known keys parses to a typed ResolvedConfig without error.
A file with an unknown top-level key is rejected with an AJV error naming the offending key.
A file missing version or with version != "1" is rejected with a message referencing the correct field.
All relative paths in templates, artifact schemas, and hook fields are resolved to absolute paths at load time; downstream modules receive only absolute paths.
An empty or syntactically invalid YAML file returns a structured parse error, not an unhandled exception.
AJV error messages include the schema path and the offending value.
review_criteria: additionalProperties:false enforced at every nested object level, not just the root.
Path resolution uses the .yoke.yml directory as the base, not process.cwd().
No I/O side effects inside the pure resolver; file reads live in the loader, not the resolver.
Version mismatch produces a message naming the current version and the received value.
Loader is synchronous (better-sqlite3 parity); no async code paths.

features.description: better-sqlite3 database open with all required PRAGMAs (journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000), schema_migrations bootstrap table created idempotently, single-writer connection plus separate read-only connection pool for dashboard reads.
acceptance_criteria: Opening the database sets all four PRAGMAs; PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'wal' on the open connection.
schema_migrations table is created with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS; opening an already-migrated database is a no-op.
Read-only connections open with { readonly: true } and cannot execute write statements.
A SQLITE_BUSY on a read connection waits up to 5000 ms before returning an error, not an instant throw.
The module exports a DbPool type with .writer and .reader() accessors; callers do not open raw Database objects.
review_criteria: PRAGMAs are set on every connection open, not just once per process lifetime.
Read-only path is enforced at the connection level (not by convention), so a reader cannot accidentally write.
busy_timeout is set before any migrations run (protects the migrations runner itself).
No global singleton database instance; DbPool is injected into all consuming modules.

features.description: DDL for all seven core tables — workflows, items, sessions, events, artifact_writes, pending_attention, prepost_runs — with all indexes including the partial index on pending_attention WHERE acknowledged_at IS NULL, and FK ON DELETE semantics per sqlite-schema.sql.
acceptance_criteria: All seven tables exist after applying migration 0001; PRAGMA table_info returns the exact columns specified in sqlite-schema.sql.
FK constraints are enforced: inserting an item with a nonexistent workflow_id raises a FOREIGN_KEY constraint error.
ON DELETE CASCADE propagates: deleting a workflow row removes all child items, sessions, events, artifact_writes, pending_attention, and prepost_runs rows.
The partial index idx_pending_attention_open is created; a query with WHERE acknowledged_at IS NULL uses it (verified via EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN).
All column types match sqlite-schema.sql exactly (TEXT, INTEGER — no VARCHAR).
No column beyond what sqlite-schema.sql specifies is present in any table.
review_criteria: Partial index WHERE acknowledged_at IS NULL is present and used by the attention banner query.
items.data column is TEXT (opaque JSON blob), not a structured column — harness must not add typed columns for item user-data fields.
prepost_runs.when_phase CHECK constraint (pre|post) is enforced.
sessions.parent_session_id FK references sessions(id), not workflows or items.

features.description: Forward-only migrations runner: discovers .sql files in src/server/storage/migrations/ ordered by integer version prefix, applies each inside a db.transaction, records the version in schema_migrations, skips already-applied versions, panics on version regression.
acceptance_criteria: Running the migrations runner on a fresh database applies 0001_initial.sql and records version 1 in schema_migrations.
Running again on a database where version 1 is already recorded skips 0001 and emits no error.
A .sql file numbered lower than the highest applied version causes a panic with a message naming both versions.
A migration that contains a SQL syntax error rolls back completely; schema_migrations is not updated for that version.
Migrations are applied in numeric order regardless of filesystem readdir order.
review_criteria: Each migration executes inside db.transaction(fn)() — no partial migration can commit on SQL error.
Version comparison is numeric (not lexicographic): version 10 sorts after version 9.
Runner refuses to apply if schema_migrations contains a version higher than any known file (forward-only guard).
No migration file is executed more than once per database.

features.description: Typed db.transaction(fn)() wrapper used by every state-transition write path; ensures atomicity for all multi-table writes; bubbles exceptions without swallowing them; no silent rollback.
acceptance_criteria: A transaction that writes to two tables (e.g. items + events) is atomic: either both rows are visible or neither is after a simulated mid-transaction crash.
An exception thrown inside fn causes the transaction to roll back; the exception is re-thrown to the caller without wrapping.
Concurrent reader connections do not see partial writes during a transaction.
Every state-transition call site uses the transaction wrapper, not raw prepare().run().
The wrapper is typed: fn receives a typed Db instance and returns T; the outer call returns T.
review_criteria: No catch block inside the wrapper that swallows exceptions.
No raw prepare().run() calls in pipeline-engine.ts, state-machine, or worktree-mgr (all go through the wrapper).
The wrapper is not a global (injected via DbPool); no module-level open transaction.

features.description: TRANSITIONS const (closed (state, event) → TransitionResult map from state-machine-transitions.md), transition(state, event) pure lookup function, and failure classifier returning transient|permanent|policy|unknown from stderr content and stream-json parse context.
acceptance_criteria: Every (state, event) pair listed in state-machine-transitions.md has a row in TRANSITIONS; an unknown pair returns undefined (does not throw).
transition() is a pure function with no side effects: identical inputs always produce identical TransitionResult.
Failure classifier tags a rate-limit API error frame as transient.
Failure classifier tags an authentication error frame as permanent.
Failure classifier returns unknown for any stderr pattern not matching a known class; unknown always routes to awaiting_user.
State and Event types are TypeScript union literals; adding a new state without updating TRANSITIONS produces a compile-time error.
review_criteria: No side effects (SQLite writes, process signals, timer creation) inside transition() or the classifier.
TRANSITIONS is a const — not mutated at runtime.
Classifier consumes both stderr string and stream-json parse state (parse_errors count, last event type); not stderr alone.
unknown classification always routes to awaiting_user — no code path where unknown leads to retry.

features.description: Full unit test suite covering all 19 assertions from state-machine-transitions.md §Test assertion list, plus a completeness test iterating the full (State × Event) cartesian product against TRANSITIONS.
acceptance_criteria: All 19 assertions from state-machine-transitions.md are present as named tests; none are skipped.
The completeness test iterates every (State × Event) pair and fails if any pair has no TRANSITIONS row.
No test mocks TRANSITIONS — all assertions run against the real const.
Assertion 6: in_progress → complete (last phase in stage) requires session_ok ∧ validators_ok ∧ diff_check_ok ∧ all post commands continue.
Assertion 13: user_cancel accepted from every non-terminal state and terminates in abandoned.
Assertion 16: replaying the crash recovery path produces zero state delta (idempotency).
All 19 tests pass without network I/O, file I/O, or SQLite access.
review_criteria: Tests use the real TRANSITIONS const, not a test-only copy.
Each assertion number from transitions.md is traceable to a named test.
Every failure-mode row in the transition table has at least one test (no happy-path-only coverage).
Tests run in under 1 second total (pure data structure assertions, no async).

features.description: JigProcessManager: command-agnostic spawn(command, args) with detached process group, stdin prompt buffer delivery, stderr capture (64 KB cap), pgid tracking, exit/error typed event emission, kill(pid,0) liveness probe, SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation on cancel (10 s grace), EPIPE handling on stdin close.
acceptance_criteria: Spawned process gets its own pgid; kill(-pgid, SIGTERM) on cancel sends SIGTERM to the entire process group.
After 10 s grace period without exit, kill(-pgid, SIGKILL) is issued; a test fixture verifies the escalation fires.
EPIPE on stdin write does not crash the harness; it is caught as a named failure class and logged.
Stderr is capped at 64 KB; lines beyond the cap are dropped with a warning, not buffered indefinitely.
ProcessManager interface is exported; JigProcessManager implements it without exposing spawn internals to callers.
No hard-coded 'claude' or 'jig' string anywhere in the spawn path — command and args come exclusively from ResolvedConfig.
review_criteria: EPIPE caught as a named failure class (not a generic catch), re-raised as a typed ProcessError.
Zombie reap handled via detached+unref pattern (not explicit wait); documented in a code comment.
Stderr cap enforced in the stream handler, not by reading all stderr into memory first.
ProcessManager interface allows both JigProcessManager and ScriptedProcessManager as drop-in replacements; no JigProcessManager-specific method leaks into the interface.
No 'claude' or 'jig' string constants in process/manager.ts or process/jig-manager.ts.

features.description: NDJSON line-buffered stream-json parser: emits typed events (stream.text, stream.thinking, stream.tool_use, stream.tool_result, stream.usage, stream.system_notice) per content block type; detects rate_limit_detected from API error frames; increments parse_errors status flag on malformed lines.
acceptance_criteria: Each stdout line is parsed independently; a malformed line logs a warning and increments status_flags.parse_errors without halting the parser.
stream.text events carry blockId and textDelta; final:true set on the last delta for a block (content_block_stop).
stream.usage event carries all four token fields: input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens.
rate_limit_detected is raised when the stream contains an API overload/rate-limit error frame; reset_at extracted if present.
Parser tested against at least one empirically captured stream-json fixture file (not a synthetic payload).
stream.tool_result always follows the matching stream.tool_use for the same toolUseId within a session.
review_criteria: Line-buffered (splits on \n only, no chunk-size assumptions); tested with a fixture arriving in arbitrary chunk sizes.
Rate-limit detection pattern tested against the empirically verified format from docs/research/.
parse_errors counter stored in sessions.status_flags JSON column, not in memory only.
No streaming parser library that buffers the entire stdout before emitting events.

features.description: Two-signal heartbeat: (1) liveness probe — kill(pid, 0) on a configurable interval, emits stream.system_notice{source:heartbeat} on stale PID; (2) stream-activity watchdog — warns after configurable idle interval with no stdout lines; neither signal ever auto-kills the process; both suppressed while item.status=rate_limited.
acceptance_criteria: Liveness probe emits a stream.system_notice warning (not error) when kill(pid,0) returns ESRCH; does not send SIGTERM.
Stream-activity watchdog emits a warning after the configured idle interval with no stdout; does not send SIGTERM.
Both probes are suppressed while the item's status is rate_limited.
Heartbeat interval is read from ResolvedConfig at construction time, not hard-coded.
Suppression state is passed in by the Pipeline Engine on each tick, not cached from a prior tick.
review_criteria: Neither probe path calls SIGTERM, SIGKILL, or any process kill signal — warnings only, always.
Suppression condition is checked at each tick, not at construction time.
Timer is cleared on session end; no leaked setInterval.

features.description: ScriptedProcessManager: replays captured stream-json JSONL fixtures (raw NDJSON lines + exit code + capped stderr) producing identical typed events to the live manager; yoke record capture mode writing to tests/fixtures/; at least one fixture per failure mode table row from plan-draft3 §Failure Modes.
acceptance_criteria: Replay of a captured fixture produces identical typed events (same sequence, same payloads) to what the live manager produces from the same raw stdout.
yoke record writes a fixture file containing: raw NDJSON lines, exit code, and capped stderr; file is self-contained for replay.
At least one fixture exists for each of: clean exit (session_ok), non-zero exit with transient classifier, non-zero exit with permanent classifier, rate_limit_detected mid-stream.
ScriptedProcessManager implements the ProcessManager interface exactly; no caller needs to know it is scripted.
Fixture format includes a version field; replay refuses an unknown version.
review_criteria: No real process spawn inside ScriptedProcessManager; all I/O comes from the fixture file.
Exit timing matches the recording (not immediate) to keep downstream timers realistic in tests.
Fixture format is documented (version field, schema comment) so contributors can author fixtures manually.
Each failure-mode fixture maps to a named row in the Failure Modes table.

features.description: Create yoke/<name>-<shortid> branch and worktree under .worktrees/; run bootstrap.commands as a state-machine bootstrap phase raising bootstrap_ok/bootstrap_fail events; invoke .yoke/teardown.sh before git worktree remove; ordered cleanup (kill pids → teardown → git worktree remove --force → branch retention); refuse auto-cleanup if branch has unpushed commits without a PR.
acceptance_criteria: Worktree created with branch name matching yoke/<workflow-name>-<shortid> pattern; branch_name and worktree_path written to the workflows row inside a transaction.
bootstrap.commands run in declared order; a non-zero exit from any command triggers bootstrap_fail event and inserts pending_attention{kind:bootstrap_failed}.
bootstrap_failed state never auto-cleans the worktree; only user_retry or user_cancel can leave it.
.yoke/teardown.sh is invoked (if present) before git worktree remove; its absence is not an error.
Auto-cleanup is refused (logs a warning, leaves the worktree) if the branch has commits not present on the remote and no PR exists for the branch.
Ordered cleanup: kill tracked pids → teardown → git worktree remove --force → branch retention decision.
review_criteria: Bootstrap treated as a state-machine phase (raises bootstrap_ok/bootstrap_fail events consumed by Pipeline Engine), not a side-effectful init routine.
Branch retention decision reads the git remote state (not just local refs).
git worktree remove uses --force only after teardown.sh has run.
Worktree path is absolute and validated to be under .worktrees/ (no path traversal).

features.description: Hand-rolled {{name}} template engine (no external library); PromptContext builder reading SQLite item row (opaque data blob + harness state), worktree files, git log (last 20 commits), and handoff entries; pure (template, ctx) → string assembler with no I/O.
acceptance_criteria: {{item}} interpolated with the opaque item.data JSON blob as a formatted string; {{item_state}} interpolated with harness state fields (status, currentPhase, retryCount).
A missing template key produces [MISSING:key] in the output string, not a throw.
Pure assembler function is unit-testable with no database or filesystem access; all context passed as a plain object.
PromptContext builder reads item.data as an opaque blob and does not access any named fields beyond what template variables reference.
Dry-run preview path (assembler called without spawning anything) produces the full prompt string.
{{handoff}} interpolated from the most-recent handoff entries for the item.
review_criteria: No I/O inside assembler.ts — file reads live in context.ts (the builder), not the assembler.
Template engine is hand-rolled (no Mustache/Handlebars dependency); {{name}} pattern only.
PromptContext builder does not access item.data fields beyond what template variables reference.
assembler.ts has complete pure-function test coverage; all tests pass without touching SQLite or the filesystem.

features.description: Append-only per-session JSONL writer under ~/.yoke/<fingerprint>/logs/<workflow-id>/<session-id>.jsonl, written by ProcessManager (stream-json line copy) and Pre/Post Runner (command output frames); survives worktree cleanup; HTTP paging endpoint GET /api/sessions/:id/log?sinceSeq=&limit= backed by this store.
acceptance_criteria: JSONL file is created on session spawn; each stream-json stdout line is copied verbatim as a log entry.
Pre/Post Runner output frames are written to the session log.
Log file survives worktree teardown and removal (lives in ~/.yoke/, not in the worktree).
sessions.session_log_path column is set to the absolute log path on session spawn.
GET /api/sessions/:id/log?sinceSeq=N&limit=M returns frames N+1..N+M in original order; 404 if session not found.
HTTP endpoint is read-only; no write or delete path exposed.
review_criteria: Writer is append-only (no seek, no random access); implemented with a streaming append file handle.
Log retention governed by config (not auto-deleted on worktree cleanup); retention policy documented.
HTTP endpoint reads from the JSONL file directly (not from SQLite); SQLite stores only the path pointer.
Log path includes a fingerprint-like component to prevent collisions between parallel workflow runs.

features.description: AJV-based validation of declared output_artifacts against their configured JSON schemas; raises validators_ok or validator_fail (with structured AJV error array) events consumed by the Pipeline Engine; each artifact validated independently with no short-circuit on first failure.
acceptance_criteria: features.json validated against features.schema.json; a missing required field produces validator_fail with the AJV error path.
validator_fail payload includes the artifact path, the schema id, and the full AJV error array (not just a string message).
validators_ok emitted only when all declared artifacts pass; a single failure suppresses validators_ok.
A required artifact that does not exist on disk raises validator_fail (not an unhandled ENOENT).
Each artifact is validated independently; a second artifact's result does not depend on the first's pass/fail.
AJV error messages are human-readable and include the schema path and the offending value.
review_criteria: AJV errors are structured (not toString'd) — callers receive the raw error array.
No schema caching between workflow runs (schemas on disk may change; always re-read at validation time).
Missing required artifact raises validator_fail, not an uncaught ENOENT.
Validator runs after session_ok and before post: commands (per hook-contract.md §2 ordering).

features.description: Three surfaces of the quality-gate contract: (1) items_from diff check — compare pre-phase snapshot vs post-session state, raise diff_check_ok or diff_check_fail; (2) artifact_writes sha256 recording — scan worktree diff after session exit, record every written file path + sha256 in artifact_writes using a streaming hash; (3) optional .yoke/last-check.json manifest reader — validate hook_version "1" shape and emit a display payload; malformed manifest is a warning only, never a phase-acceptance condition.
acceptance_criteria: diff_check_fail raised when the items_from file has any non-whitespace change between pre-phase snapshot and post-session state; diff summary included in the event payload.
diff_check_ok raised when the file is unchanged.
sha256 of every file written by the session recorded in artifact_writes; computed via streaming hash.
.yoke/last-check.json absence treated as normal (no event raised); presence triggers shape validation.
A malformed .yoke/last-check.json emits stream.system_notice{source:"hook", severity:"warn"} and does not affect phase acceptance under any condition.
hook_version != "1" in last-check.json renders a warning badge payload and passes through raw JSON.
review_criteria: Diff check compares a pre-phase snapshot taken before the agent session spawns (not git history).
sha256 computed with a streaming hash — no large-file buffering into memory.
Manifest validation does not block phase acceptance under any condition (display-only by contract).
artifact_writes rows inserted inside the same db.transaction as the post-session state transition.
No Claude hook installation, no checksum verification of .claude/hooks/ files — explicitly out of scope per hook-contract.md §4.

features.description: Pipeline engine: read .yoke.yml stage graph, schedule items in topological order, drive state-machine transitions via SQLite transactions, advance phases within a stage, advance stages (stage_complete event), retry ladder (continue → fresh_with_failure_summary → awaiting_user), cascade-block transitive dependents in one transaction, crash recovery restart projection on startup.
acceptance_criteria: Stage advancement fires stage_complete event only when ALL items in the stage are terminal (complete, blocked, or abandoned); a single non-terminal item prevents advancement.
Dependency cascade-blocks all transitive dependents in the same SQLite transaction when an item transitions to awaiting_user, blocked, or abandoned.
Retry ladder applies in order: attempt 1 = continue (-c), attempt 2 = fresh_with_failure_summary, attempt 3 = awaiting_user; configurable max_outer_retries per phase.
Crash recovery: rebuilds projection from SQLite on startup, probes stale PIDs via kill(pid,0), sets recovery_state JSON on affected workflows, does not apply new transitions.
Pipeline Engine is the only component that mutates SQLite workflow/item/session state; no other module calls db.writer directly.
Every transition is committed inside db.transaction before any observable external side effect (process spawn, WS frame emission).
review_criteria: No long-lived in-memory workflow state; every scheduling decision re-reads SQLite.
Crash recovery sets recovery_state only — no auto-transition; user_retry or auto_resume fires the normal (awaiting_user, user_retry) → in_progress row.
max_revisits guard for goto actions tracked per (item_id, destination_phase) pair, not globally.
Stage with needs_approval:true inserts pending_attention{kind:stage_needs_approval} and pauses until stage_approval_granted.
events table row written for every transition with full correlation set: workflow_id, item_id, session_id, stage, phase, attempt.

features.description: Pre/Post command runner: spawn(cmd, args, {shell:false}) in worktree CWD, per-command wall-clock timeout (default 15 min, SIGTERM+10s+SIGKILL escalation), action grammar evaluation (exit-code → action), max_revisits guard for goto actions, stdout/stderr captured to session log + WS prepost.command.* frames, one prepost_runs row per command per attempt.
acceptance_criteria: shell:false used for all pre and post command spawns; shell metacharacters in argv are not interpreted.
A pre: command with a non-continue action blocks the agent session from spawning; the resolved action is handed to the Pipeline Engine.
post: commands run only after session_ok (not after session_fail); action resolution hands the resolved action to the Pipeline Engine for execution.
A missing wildcard '*' key in actions is a config-validation error raised at load time (not at runtime).
max_revisits guard fires when a goto action exceeds the configured limit and routes to awaiting_user.
Each command execution writes a prepost_runs row with started_at, ended_at, exit_code, and action_taken.
review_criteria: shell:false enforced — no hidden shell invocation via /bin/sh -c.
Action execution (goto, retry, stop, fail) lives in the Pipeline Engine, not in the runner.
Absent '*' key caught at config load time (feat-config-loader), not at action evaluation time.
prepost_runs rows inserted inside the same db.transaction as the corresponding state transition.
Command timeout uses SIGTERM + 10 s grace + SIGKILL (same escalation as process manager).

features.description: Orchestration loop wired into yoke start: on startup calls buildCrashRecovery then enters a poll/event loop that reads SQLite for runnable items, assembles prompts via PromptAssembler, creates worktrees via WorktreeManager, spawns sessions via ProcessManager, feeds stdout lines through StreamJsonParser, fires applyItemTransition on every classifier event, runs pre/post commands via PrePostRunner, and broadcasts state changes as WS frames — the glue layer that makes all existing building blocks drive real workflow execution.
acceptance_criteria: yoke start on a repo with a valid .yoke.yml ingests the stage graph, upserts workflow + item rows into SQLite, calls buildCrashRecovery, and begins scheduling within 2 seconds of startup.
Items whose depends_on are all terminal-complete are picked up and transitioned to in_progress before ProcessManager.spawn is called; items with unmet dependencies are skipped.

For each spawned session: prompt assembled via assemblePrompt, worktree created via WorktreeManager.createWorktree, process spawned via ProcessManager.spawn, each stdout_line fed to StreamJsonParser, classifier events forwarded to applyItemTransition.
Pre-phase commands run via PrePostRunner before session spawn; a non-continue pre action blocks the spawn and hands the resolved action to applyItemTransition.
Post-phase commands run via PrePostRunner after session_ok; a non-continue post action is handed to applyItemTransition (not executed by the runner).
rate_limit_detected from StreamJsonParser transitions the item to rate_limited and schedules a retry after the reset_at timestamp; no spawn occurs during the backoff window.
WS subscribers receive incremental state-change frames for every applyItemTransition the scheduler fires; no subscriber polling required.
On SIGINT/SIGTERM the scheduler drains in-flight sessions (cancel + wait up to gracePeriodMs) before process.exit.
review_criteria: Every scheduling decision re-reads SQLite — no in-memory workflow cache between loop ticks.
All SQLite mutations go through applyItemTransition; no direct db.writer calls in the scheduler itself.
Process spawn happens only after the in_progress transition is committed (applyItemTransition returns before spawn).
buildCrashRecovery called on startup before any new items are scheduled; stale sessions do not get double-spawned.
Concurrency limit (max_parallel from ResolvedConfig or a sensible default) enforced by counting running sessions from SQLite, not an in-memory counter.

Scheduler is a class with an injectable ProcessManager, WorktreeManager, PromptAssembler, and PrePostRunner so the integration test suite can swap in scripted/fake implementations without touching yoke start.

features.description: FaultInjector checkpoint seam injected into Pipeline Engine, ProcessManager, and WorktreeManager at named checkpoints (bootstrap_ok, session_ok, post_commands_ok, artifact_validators); no-op in production (gated by env flag); used by crash-recovery test suite to exercise every restart projection path without manual intervention.
acceptance_criteria: FaultInjector is no-op when YOKE_FAULT_INJECT env var is not set; zero overhead in the production path.
Fault injection at bootstrap_ok checkpoint triggers the bootstrap_fail recovery path in a test.
Fault injection at session_ok checkpoint triggers the crash recovery restart projection path.
Crash-recovery test suite exercises: stale PID at restart, item in_progress at restart, item rate_limited at restart — all with zero manual intervention.
Each checkpoint named in the FaultInjector API (string enum, not positional); test fixtures reference checkpoints by name.
review_criteria: Seam requires zero production code changes beyond injection call-sites; no global state mutation.
Each checkpoint is a named string enum (not positional) so test authors can target specific failure points.
No sleep() hacks needed to trigger recovery paths; fault injection is synchronous.
FaultInjector is injected via constructor parameter (not a process.env lookup inside the class).

features.description: Fastify HTTP + WebSocket server binding 127.0.0.1: hello frame + protocol version check (close 4001 on mismatch), subscribe/unsubscribe/backfill/backfill.truncated, control frame idempotency (commandId, 5 min window), all ServerFrame types with per-session monotonic seq, subscription cap (4 per client), all HTTP companion endpoints from protocol-websocket.md §7.
acceptance_criteria: hello frame sent immediately on connect with protocolVersion:1 and capabilities array; a client with protocolVersion != 1 receives close code 4001.
subscribe triggers workflow.snapshot followed by backfill frames (original seq preserved); backfill.truncated emitted with httpFetchUrl when sinceSeq is too old.
Subscription cap of 4 concurrent streaming workflows per client enforced; a 5th subscribe receives error{code:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT} and close 4002.
control commandId idempotency: repeating the same id within 5 min returns the previous response without re-executing the action.
All HTTP endpoints from protocol-websocket.md §7 return correct 2xx shape for valid input, 404 for unknown workflow, 400 for bad query params.
Server binds 127.0.0.1 exclusively; no 0.0.0.0 bind path exists.
review_criteria: Binds 127.0.0.1 only — no 0.0.0.0 code path anywhere.
seq is monotonic per-session starting at 1; non-session frames use seq:0; backfill replays preserve original seq.
WS and HTTP both read from a SQLite read-only connection; no writes from the API layer.
control commandId idempotency enforced in memory or SQLite — not by re-executing and comparing results.
No authentication middleware — explicitly out of scope per D57.

features.description: Octokit auto-PR creation, gh CLI fallback when octokit auth fails, auth resolution order (GITHUB_TOKEN → gh auth status → structured error), push-before-PR guard (always enforced, not config-gated), GithubState enum columns written to workflows row.
acceptance_criteria: PR created via octokit when GITHUB_TOKEN is set and valid; GithubState transitions to creating → created with prNumber and prUrl populated.
When GITHUB_TOKEN is absent or invalid, falls back to gh CLI; gh auth status failure produces a structured error (not a stack trace).
push-before-PR guard refuses PR creation if the branch has unpushed commits; guard enforced regardless of config.
Auth resolution failure emits a structured error naming which paths were attempted (GITHUB_TOKEN, gh auth) and why each failed.
Both octokit and gh paths tested with scripted fixtures (no live GitHub API calls in CI).
review_criteria: push-before-PR guard always enforced — not config-gated.
Both octokit and gh paths tested with scripted fixtures; no live GitHub API calls in unit or integration tests.
Structured error on auth failure includes which paths were attempted.
GithubState columns updated inside a db.transaction together with any related events row.

features.description: Notification dispatch service: pending_attention as authoritative source; info severity logs to console only; requires_attention severity fires node-notifier macOS native notification with workflow deep-link URL; browser-push POST stub returns 501 Not Implemented; no notification emitted without a pending_attention row.
acceptance_criteria: requires_attention severity triggers a node-notifier notification with the workflow name and a deep-link URL to the correct workflow.
info severity logs to console only; no native notification emitted.
Browser-push POST endpoint returns HTTP 501 Not Implemented with a JSON body explaining stub status.
No notification is emitted without a corresponding pending_attention row in SQLite.
Notification dispatch reads pending_attention at emission time, not from an in-memory cache.
review_criteria: pending_attention is the source of truth; notification is a side effect of inserting the row, not the source.
Browser-push stub does not attempt VAPID key generation or real web push delivery.
node-notifier dependency gracefully skipped on non-macOS or missing binary (no crash).
No notification sent without a pending_attention row — enforced by reading the row before dispatch.

features.description: CLI subcommands via commander: yoke init (opt-in .yoke.yml + example prompts scaffold, never overwrites existing files), yoke start (spawn pipeline engine), yoke status (poll workflow state, print table), yoke cancel (send control frame with fresh commandId), yoke doctor (check Node >= 20, SQLite, git >= 2.20, config validity with actionable per-check output), yoke record (wire ScriptedProcessManager capture mode).
acceptance_criteria: yoke init creates .yoke.yml and example prompt templates on opt-in; exits with an error (not overwrite) if any target file already exists.
yoke start spawns the pipeline engine and logs the server URL; exits non-zero if config validation fails.
yoke status polls GET /api/workflows and prints a table with id, name, status, current_stage, and active session count.
yoke cancel sends POST /api/workflows/:id/control with action=cancel and a fresh UUID commandId; prints confirmation.
yoke doctor checks Node >= 20, SQLite accessible, git >= 2.20, .yoke.yml valid; prints a pass/fail row per check with actionable remediation text.
yoke record wires ScriptedProcessManager capture mode for the next session.
review_criteria: yoke init never overwrites existing files — hardcoded check, not a --force flag default.
yoke doctor produces actionable remediation text per failed check (not just 'failed').
yoke cancel generates a fresh UUID commandId per invocation for idempotency.
All subcommands handle ECONNREFUSED (server not running) with a clear human-readable error.
No shell-injection risk in any CLI argument passed to child_process.

features.description: Wire AckAttentionFn into start.ts: createServer receives an acknowledgement callback connected to the engine-layer handler so that POST /api/workflows/:id/attention/:attentionId/ack correctly transitions the pending_attention row from unacknowledged to acknowledged and broadcasts a WS state update.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/workflows/:id/attention/:attentionId/ack returns 200 (not 501) when a pending_attention row exists with that id.
The acknowledged_at column is set and the row disappears from the partial index (WHERE acknowledged_at IS NULL).
A WS frame is broadcast after acknowledgement.
review_criteria: createServer() receives a non-stub AckAttentionFn in production start.ts.
Unit test covers the ack path without a full server (injects a mock ack fn).

features.description: Guard startServer and yoke doctor against being run outside a git repository: call `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` at startup and exit with a clear actionable error if it fails. WorktreeManager requires a real git repo at config.configDir and will throw uninformative errors without this guard.
acceptance_criteria: yoke start in a non-git directory exits non-zero with a message naming the missing requirement.
yoke doctor reports a FAIL check for the git-repo requirement with an actionable fix hint.
No change to behaviour when run inside a valid git repo.
review_criteria: Check is in startServer (not buried inside WorktreeManager where the error surface is already mid-flight).
Error message includes the config.configDir path and the git command that failed.

features.description: Wire the yoke record capture marker into the scheduler's session-spawn path: read readRecordMarker() before spawning; if enabled, tee each stdout_line from JigSpawnHandle to the fixture file as {type:'stdout',line} NDJSON, write {type:'exit',code} on exit, call clearRecordMarker() after the session ends. This makes `yoke record <fixture>` actually capture a session.
acceptance_criteria: Running `yoke record /tmp/fixture.jsonl && yoke start` produces a valid fixture file that ScriptedProcessManager can replay.
clearRecordMarker() is called after the session ends (even on error), so a subsequent yoke start does not re-enter capture mode.
The fixture file contains stdout lines and a terminal exit record.
review_criteria: Capture tee logic is in the scheduler's _runSession path, not inside JigProcessManager itself.
Test uses ScriptedProcessManager to replay the captured fixture and asserts identical event sequence.

features.description: Implement lazy per-item stage seeding: when a per-item stage's placeholder item transitions to ready, read the items_from manifest from the worktree, evaluate items_list (JSONPath) to extract the array, create one SQLite item row per manifest entry using items_id for the stable ID, evaluate items_complete per item and seed as status:'complete' if truthy (skips already-done items), evaluate items_depends_on per item for topological ordering. Use jsonpath-plus for all JSONPath expressions so filter predicates work. Replace the placeholder item with the real seeded items; the stage does not advance until all seeded items reach terminal states.
acceptance_criteria: A per-item stage with 3 manifest entries produces 3 SQLite item rows (not a placeholder).
Items whose items_complete expression returns a truthy value are seeded with status:'complete' and never spawned by the scheduler.
items_depends_on topological ordering is respected: item B (depends_on: [A]) is not scheduled until A is complete.
items_list JSONPath filter predicates work: '$.features[?(@.priority > 1)]' seeds only matching items.
The placeholder item row is removed or superseded by the real item rows; the stage completion check counts real items only.
review_criteria: jsonpath-plus is used for all JSONPath evaluation (not hand-rolled); it handles filter predicates correctly.
items_complete is evaluated at seed time only, not on every tick; SQLite status is the runtime source of truth after seeding.
Seeding is idempotent: re-seeding the same stage (e.g. on crash recovery) does not create duplicate item rows.
Unit tests cover: empty manifest, all-complete manifest, partial completion, dependency ordering, filter expression, missing items_complete field.

features.description: Human-readable generated name per workflow run: migration 0003 adds display_name TEXT column to workflows table, word-list generator (src/server/util/generate-name.ts) produces {adjective}-{gerund}-{noun} names, ingestWorkflow populates display_name on new workflow creation only (not resume), PATCH /api/workflows/:id allows user override (alphanumeric+hyphens, max 64 chars), yoke status CLI shows display_name. WS broadcast on rename.
acceptance_criteria: A new workflow row has a non-null display_name auto-populated from the generator; the name matches {word}-{word}-{word} pattern.
Resuming an existing workflow does not change display_name.
PATCH /api/workflows/:id with { displayName: 'my-name' } updates display_name and returns 200 with the updated workflow.
PATCH with an empty string, >64 chars, or invalid characters (not alphanumeric+hyphens) returns 400 with a descriptive error.
PATCH with the same displayName that's already set returns 200 (idempotent, no WS broadcast).
WS broadcast of workflow.update is sent to all subscribers after a successful rename.
yoke status CLI table shows display_name column (or UUID prefix if null for pre-migration rows).
review_criteria: Generator is a standalone pure function with no I/O; word list is embedded in the module (not a file read).
Migration 0003 uses ALTER TABLE (not DROP/CREATE) so existing workflow rows keep their data.
PATCH endpoint validates display_name before writing; no raw user input reaches SQLite without validation.
display_name uniqueness is NOT enforced — collisions are fine (UUIDs are the real key).
ingestWorkflow resume path reads and returns existing display_name without touching it.

features.description: Workflow identity redesign: each yoke start writes .yoke/runs/<uuid>.json (not git-tracked) as the workflow instance pointer; ingestWorkflow receives explicit { workflowId, isResume } from the caller instead of deriving from (project.name, configDir); yoke start checks for alive PIDs (refuse if alive) and orphaned files (auto-resume); scheduler archives run file on terminal state; yoke status reads .yoke/runs/ for local context.
acceptance_criteria: yoke start with no active run file creates .yoke/runs/<uuid>.json containing { workflowId, displayName, configDir, pid, startedAt }.
yoke start when a run file exists with a live PID exits non-zero with a message naming the active workflow's displayName.
yoke start when a run file exists with a dead PID auto-resumes that workflow, updates the run file's pid, and logs 'Resuming orphaned workflow: {displayName}'.
ingestWorkflow called with { workflowId, isResume: false } always INSERTs a new workflows row with the provided UUID.
ingestWorkflow called with { workflowId, isResume: true } SELECTs the existing row and returns isResume:true; errors if not found or already terminal.
When the scheduler transitions a workflow to a terminal state, the run file is moved from .yoke/runs/ to .yoke/archive/.
yoke status shows display_name and status from .yoke/runs/ files for active workflows.
review_criteria: The (project.name, configDir) SELECT query is completely removed from ingest.ts — no fallback path.
.yoke/runs/ and .yoke/archive/ are listed in .gitignore (via the existing .yoke/ entry).
PID liveness check uses kill(pid, 0) — same pattern as buildCrashRecovery.
ingestWorkflow tests use explicit { workflowId, isResume } — no tests rely on the old (name, configDir) lookup.
Scheduler archiveRunFile is called inside the terminal-state transition handler, not in a separate cleanup pass.
